pattern: Only how many stone values fall into each residue class modulo 3 decides the winner of this removal game, never the actual values or their order.
hints: Group the stones by their remainder when divided by 3 — only the three group sizes matter from here on.
The parity of how many stones have remainder 0 changes which comparison between the remainder-1 and remainder-2 counts decides the winner.
If the remainder-0 count is even, Alice wins exactly when both the remainder-1 and remainder-2 counts are at least 1; if it is odd, Alice wins exactly when those two counts differ by more than 2.

edge_inputs.description: single stone divisible by three, Alice must take the loss
single stone not divisible by three, board empties on Bob's turn
all stones share the same remainder, even count of remainder-zero stones
zero remainder-zero stones but one of the other groups is empty
balanced non-zero groups with an even remainder-zero count
odd remainder-zero count with a large imbalance between the other groups
odd remainder-zero count with the other groups exactly two apart

stress.description: large random stone values across the full range
